location.office_time_required: Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday in-office; Wednesday and Friday may vary

level_signals: Owns product strategy and long-term roadmap
Operates independently in highly ambiguous problem spaces
Base salary range $176,000–$200,000 CAD consistent with Senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of product management experience managing complex backend systems, infrastructure, or platform products, ideally with a background in engineering or a technical transition into product management.
Deep understanding of global payment systems, billing administration, pricing/packaging configurations, and financial data pipelines.
Proven track record operating independently in highly ambiguous problem spaces, balancing deep technical debt/investments against immediate business needs.
Exceptional ability to simplify system-level and integration-level complexity into clear, sequenced architectural bets.
Excellent cross-functional communication style, with a talent for building shared understanding across deeply technical and operational business teams.
Demonstrates curiosity about AI tools and emerging technologies, with a willingness to learn and leverage them to enhance productivity, collaboration, or decision-making.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: ideally with a background in engineering or a technical transition into product management.

scope.surface: Payment Platform — core monetization infrastructure including billing administration, product catalogue, pricing/packaging, and incentive workflows
core_responsibilities: Own the product strategy and long-term roadmap for Asana's monetization platform
Shift capabilities from siloed, long-cycle features into reusable, self-service components, patterns, and APIs
Ensure billing administration, product catalogue, and incentive workflows are highly reliable, scalable, and technically robust
Partner with Product Engineering, Infrastructure, Finance, and Data teams to streamline revenue-critical operational workflows
Define durable success metrics and indicators such as expansion, activation, and billing retention

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of compensation package

green_flags: Clear product surface and ownership scope (monetization platform)
Explicit hybrid schedule details provided upfront
Named cross-functional partners (Engineering, Infrastructure, Finance, Data) indicate structured org
Yearly pay equity audit demonstrates compensation transparency commitment
red_flags: Role is Vancouver office-based only — eliminates remote candidates and requires Canadian work authorization
Work authorization and visa sponsorship not stated — risky for international candidates
Base salary stated in CAD, not USD — may be misread without currency context
Friday in-office policy is ambiguous: depends on role type and team

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years requirement, scope of ownership, and compensation range)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description — B2B SaaS subscription)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; partial quote from JD but assembled as inference)
compensation.base_range_usd (not stated; compensation is in CAD — left as null for USD field)
ambiguities: The JD body says Friday in-office 'depends on the type of work you do and the teams with which you partner' — not a fixed policy, ambiguous for candidates planning schedules.

Engineering background qualifier ('ideally with a background in engineering') appears in the same bullet as the hard 5+ years requirement, making it unclear if it is truly optional or implicitly required. Treated as nice-to-have (soft) here.
Compensation is in CAD; the schema base_range_usd field is null. Added non-schema fields base_range_local and base_range_currency to preserve the stated data faithfully.
